method_atom: purely relaxational (non-conserved order parameter) stochastic dynamics of a many-body system with quenched randomness near a continuous phase transition, characterised by a universal dynamic critical exponent that ties the divergence of autocorrelation/relaxation timescales to system size, estimated by Monte Carlo sampling both in equilibrium and off-equilibrium after a sudden quench of the control parameter across its critical value
object_atom: three-dimensional disordered Ising magnets — site-diluted, bond-diluted, and random-bond (+-J) spin models on a lattice along the paramagnetic-ferromagnetic transition line
brief_reason: the relaxational critical-dynamics machinery with its universal dynamic exponent carries across any disordered system at a continuous transition, while the diluted/random-bond Ising spin lattices are the condensed-matter-specific object.
